opportunity_name: Hawaii Community Foundation - Community Grants Program
deadline: Multiple rounds: January, April, October annually
mission_program_area: Youth development, children and youth, community needs
brief_justification: Premier Hawaii funder with strong youth focus. Multiple rounds per year. Previously funded Maui Junior Golf ($25K). Excellent fit for youth athletics programs.
special_notes: Apply through grants portal. Must be Hawaii 501(c)(3). Strong local presence essential.

opportunity_name: Atherton Family Foundation - Capital Grants
funder_name: Atherton Family Foundation (via Hawaii Community Foundation)
mission_program_area: Youth development, education, community development, capital projects
brief_justification: CRITICAL FOR FACILITY: Just funded $200K for Mid-Pacific track/field facility in 2024. Average capital grant $38K. Perfect for facility acquisition priority.
special_notes: HIGHEST PRIORITY - Recent track/field facility grant precedent. Apply through Hawaii Community Foundation portal.

opportunity_name: Harold K.L. Castle Foundation - Strengthening Windward Oahu Communities
mission_program_area: Youth development, education, community strengthening
brief_justification: Largest private foundation in Hawaii (~$7M/year). Youth focus through Windward Youth Leadership Fund. May require relationship building.
special_notes: Windward Oahu geographic priority. Consider if Ka Ulukoa has presence in Windward area.

opportunity_name: NFL Foundation Grassroots Program
mission_program_area: Football field development and refurbishment, youth sports facilities
brief_justification: FACILITY FOCUS: Up to $250K for capital improvements. Requires 1:1 match. $65M+ distributed since inception. Could support multi-sport facility with football component.
special_notes: Must include football field component. Hawaii not typical NFL market but Hawaii Pro Bowl connection may help.

opportunity_name: USATF Foundation Youth Club Grants
mission_program_area: Youth track and field programs, equipment, travel, competitions
brief_justification: DIRECT MISSION MATCH: 46 youth clubs funded in 2025. Record $97,800 awarded in 2024. Perfect for track/field youth programs. Smaller grants but high success rate.
special_notes: Preference for clubs in low-income areas. Can cover equipment, travel, meet fees, uniforms.

opportunity_name: Laureus Sport for Good USA Grants
funder_name: Laureus Sport for Good Foundation USA
geographic_restrictions: National - focus cities: Atlanta, Chicago, New Orleans, NYC
mission_program_area: Sports-based youth development, social impact through sport
brief_justification: $33M invested since 2012. Strong youth sports focus but Hawaii not a focus city. 130+ grantees in 2024. Worth inquiry about expansion.
special_notes: Currently invite-only. Contact to express interest for future expansion to Hawaii.

opportunity_name: Women's Sports Foundation - Sports 4 Life Program
mission_program_area: Girls in sports, underserved communities, sports-based youth development
brief_justification: Surpassed $3M in grants. 40 organizations funded in 2025 ($400K total). If Ka Ulukoa serves female athletes, strong fit.
special_notes: Must serve girls in underserved communities. Co-founded with ESPN.

opportunity_name: Women's Sports Foundation - Travel & Training Fund
mission_program_area: Female athlete travel, coaching, equipment, specialized training
brief_justification: Perfect for supporting female youth athletes traveling to national/international competitions - aligns with Ka Ulukoa mission.
special_notes: For individual female athletes with Olympic/elite potential.

opportunity_name: DICK'S Foundation Sports Matter Grants
deadline: Rolling / Regional cycles (New England: Feb 5 - May 30, 2025)
mission_program_area: Youth sports equipment, registration fees, league costs
brief_justification: $100M+ committed since 2014. 2M+ kids helped. Equipment and registration focus - good for program operations, not facility.
special_notes: Not for facility/capital projects. Great for equipment and program costs.

opportunity_name: DICK'S Foundation / LISC - Game On Community Places to Play
mission_program_area: Multi-use sports facility renovation for youth ages 6-24
brief_justification: FACILITY FOCUS: ~$3M deployed in 35 facilities. Requires 1:1 match. Courts, nets, lights, bleachers. Check if Hawaii in target markets.
special_notes: Must be in low-to-moderate income community. Check 10 target markets list.

opportunity_name: Nike Community Impact Fund
geographic_restrictions: Portland metro, Northern Willamette Valley, SW Washington
mission_program_area: Youth sports, physical activity, community health
brief_justification: $550K+ annually but geographically restricted to Pacific Northwest. Not directly applicable to Hawaii.
special_notes: NOT ELIGIBLE - geographic restriction to Oregon/Washington area only.

opportunity_name: Under Armour Community Impact Grants
mission_program_area: Youth sports programs, underserved communities, athletic development
brief_justification: Supports youth sports teams, community orgs, schools. Partners with Positive Coaching Alliance. May require relationship building.
special_notes: Focus on Baltimore/Project Rampart. Inquiry needed for Hawaii opportunities.

opportunity_name: NBA Foundation Grants
mission_program_area: Economic empowerment for Black youth ages 14-24, workforce development
brief_justification: $140M+ committed. Focus on workforce development, NOT sports programs. Poor fit unless Ka Ulukoa has career/employment components.
special_notes: Must target Black youth 14-24 and focus on employment pathways. Sports not primary focus.

opportunity_name: MLB-MLBPA Youth Development Foundation Grant
grant_size_range: Up to $5,000 for equipment; larger for capital projects
mission_program_area: Youth baseball/softball programs, field renovations, education
brief_justification: FACILITY: Can fund field renovations and capital projects. But baseball/softball specific. Less relevant unless Ka Ulukoa includes these sports.
special_notes: Does NOT fund operating expenses. Focus on underserved/minority youth.

opportunity_name: USTA Tennis Venue Services (TVS) Grants
mission_program_area: Tennis court construction, renovation, lighting, resurfacing
brief_justification: FACILITY: $10M additional funding announced. $19M+ awarded since 2005. Must have public tennis courts. Tennis-specific.
special_notes: Facilities must be open to public. Only applicable if facility includes tennis courts.

opportunity_name: U.S. Soccer Foundation Safe Places to Play Grants
mission_program_area: Soccer field construction, mini-pitches, lighting, facility enhancement
brief_justification: FACILITY: 800+ mini-pitches built. Field construction, lighting, irrigation. Soccer-specific but could support multi-sport facility.
special_notes: Mini-pitches ideal for space-limited areas. 87% programs at free/reduced rate.

opportunity_name: Land and Water Conservation Fund (LWCF) - State Grants
deadline: Varies by state - contact Hawaii DLNR
mission_program_area: Outdoor recreation facilities, parks, athletic fields
brief_justification: FACILITY: Major federal program. Hawaii has received LWCF for tennis/basketball courts, playgrounds, trails. 50% match required. EXPLORE Act (2025) expands tribal/Native Hawaiian access.
special_notes: Contact Hawaii DLNR Division of State Parks for current cycle. Long application process.

opportunity_name: USDA Community Facilities Grant Program
grant_size_range: Up to $50,000 grant; larger loans available
mission_program_area: Community facilities including recreation centers, gymnasiums
brief_justification: FACILITY: Supports sports complexes, gyms, recreation facilities in rural areas. Grant max $50K but larger loans available at favorable terms.
special_notes: Must be in rural area <20,000 pop. Parts of Hawaii qualify. Contact Hawaii Rural Development office.

opportunity_name: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) - Hawaii Counties
deadline: Varies by county (typically Sept-Nov for following year)
mission_program_area: Recreation facilities, public facilities, community development
brief_justification: FACILITY: Can fund recreation facilities. Maui ~$1.87M for PY2025. Must benefit low/mod income areas. Each county has own process.
special_notes: Apply through county housing/community development offices. Honolulu 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan being developed.

opportunity_name: National Park Service - Outdoor Recreation Legacy Partnership Program (ORLP)
mission_program_area: Outdoor recreation in urban, low-income areas
brief_justification: FACILITY: Large grants for urban outdoor recreation. Through state agencies. Long timeline but significant funding potential.
special_notes: Apply through Hawaii state agency. 5-year funding window.

opportunity_name: ESPN Take Back Sports Initiative Grants
mission_program_area: Youth sports access, coach training, innovative programming
brief_justification: $5M investment in youth sports. Innovation Challenge grants. Partners with WSF, Special Olympics, Boys & Girls Clubs. Apply through partners.
special_notes: Apply through partner organizations (WSF, Positive Coaching Alliance, NRPA).

opportunity_name: The Skatepark Project (Tony Hawk Foundation) Grants
grant_size_range: Up to $25,000 (up to $250,000 with partner match)
mission_program_area: Public skatepark construction in underserved areas
brief_justification: FACILITY: $13M awarded for ~700 skateparks. Must be PUBLIC property. Not directly relevant unless facility includes skate component.
special_notes: Property must be public or donated to public entity.

opportunity_name: US Olympic & Paralympic Foundation - Team USA Community Champions
mission_program_area: Health, wellness, youth sports through Team USA athletes
brief_justification: 4 grants annually. Must partner with Team USA athlete. If Ka Ulukoa has connection to Olympic athletes, good fit.
special_notes: Requires athlete partnership. Consider Hawaiian Olympic athletes connection.

opportunity_name: Good Sports Equipment Grants
mission_program_area: Sports equipment for youth in underserved communities
brief_justification: Provides equipment directly. DICK'S Foundation gave $2M to Good Sports in 2024 (73K pieces to 44K kids). Equipment only, not facility.
special_notes: Equipment only - not cash grants. Complements facility funding efforts.

opportunity_name: Every Kid Sports Pass
mission_program_area: Youth sports registration fees for qualifying families
brief_justification: Pays registration fees for qualifying low-income families. Since 2010. Good for participant access, not organizational funding.
special_notes: For individual families, not organizational grants. Can help participants afford Ka Ulukoa programs.

opportunity_name: All Kids Play Youth Sports Grants
mission_program_area: Registration, equipment for K-12 recreational sports
brief_justification: For families AND organizations in low-income communities. Not for travel/elite/AAU sports - recreational focus.
special_notes: Does NOT fund travel, elite, or AAU programs. Recreational level only.

opportunity_name: National Council of Youth Sports (NCYS) Grants
mission_program_area: Youth sports program costs and equipment
brief_justification: $5K grants for program costs and equipment. NCYS membership may be required. Smaller but accessible.
special_notes: Consider NCYS membership for networking and grant access.

opportunity_name: Gatorade Player of the Year Community Grant
mission_program_area: Youth sports organizations nominated by POY winners
brief_justification: $5.6M+ awarded through 2,000+ orgs. Must be nominated by Gatorade POY winner. Connect with Hawaii high school athletes.
special_notes: Requires connection to Gatorade Player of the Year winners.

opportunity_name: Gatorade Equity in Sports Grant (Move United)
mission_program_area: Adaptive sports, inclusive PE, high school athletics
brief_justification: 5 programs selected annually. Must be Move United member. Focus on adaptive/inclusive sports in high schools.
special_notes: Only for adaptive sports programs. Consider if Ka Ulukoa has inclusive athletics component.

opportunity_name: Foot Locker Foundation Community Empowerment Program
mission_program_area: Youth programming and capital improvements
brief_justification: FACILITY: ~$5.2M deployed. Grants for youth programming ($25K-$75K) AND capital improvements ($25K-$100K). 5th round in 2025.
special_notes: Two grant types: programming and capital. Apply for capital improvement track.

opportunity_name: Hawaii County Waiwai Grants-in-Aid
mission_program_area: Nonprofit programs benefiting public
brief_justification: Local government grant program. $2.5M+ annually. Must benefit Big Island residents. Check if Ka Ulukoa has Big Island presence.
special_notes: County-specific. Coordinate with Hawaii County Council.

opportunity_name: Hawaii State Recreational Trails Program
mission_program_area: Non-motorized trails and recreation routes
brief_justification: FACILITY: For trails/outdoor recreation. Less direct fit but could support outdoor training facilities.
special_notes: More suited for trail-based activities. Contact DLNR for athletics facility options.

opportunity_name: 15 and the Mahomies Foundation Grants
mission_program_area: Youth health, wellness, athletics, academics
brief_justification: NFL Superbowl champion's foundation. Supports youth athletics. National scope but KC focus. Worth inquiry.
source_of_information: Web research - various youth sports grant lists
special_notes: Kansas City geographic preference. Inquiry recommended for Hawaii.

opportunity_name: JD Finish Line Foundation - Louder Than Words Grant
mission_program_area: Health & wellness, workforce development, safe communities
brief_justification: Up to $10K grants. Health/wellness focus fits athletics. Must serve people of color and demonstrate cultural competency.
special_notes: Diversity requirement - must serve people of color.

opportunity_name: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation Sports Award
mission_program_area: Health equity through sports, community health, youth wellness
brief_justification: Prestigious annual prize. Past winners include youth gymnastics, hockey, basketball programs serving underserved youth.
special_notes: Award/prize format - honorees typically well-established programs with proven impact.

opportunity_name: NRPA Game-Changing Grants for Equitable Youth Sports
funder_name: National Recreation and Park Association / Nike / ESPN
mission_program_area: Youth sports access, coach development, equity in sports
brief_justification: Nike/ESPN partnership. $20K grants include Musco Lighting donation. For park/rec agencies - may require partnership.
special_notes: Primarily for park/recreation agencies. Ka Ulukoa would need agency partnership.

opportunity_name: Dream Quest Youth Center Foundation Model
mission_program_area: Youth centers, facilities, community spaces
website_application_link: Research via ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer / Foundation Directory
brief_justification: FACILITY: Database shows large grants to youth centers ($866K to Dream Quest). Research foundations that funded similar organizations.
special_notes: Requires foundation research. Use Candid/Foundation Directory for detailed prospecting.

opportunity_name: American Athletic Union (AAU) District Scholarships/Grants
mission_program_area: Youth athlete development, education, sports access
brief_justification: DIRECT FIT: Youth athletics focus. AAU membership gives access to competitions and grants. 720,000+ members.
special_notes: Check Hawaii AAU district for local opportunities. AAU membership recommended.

opportunity_name: Positive Coaching Alliance Partnership Grants
mission_program_area: Coach training, positive youth development through sports
brief_justification: Training and resources for coaches. Under Armour partner. Could strengthen Ka Ulukoa coach development component.
special_notes: Focus on coach training. Partnership model rather than direct grant.

opportunity_name: Special Olympics Unified Sports Grants
mission_program_area: Inclusive sports, unified athletics for all abilities
brief_justification: ESPN Take Back Sports partner. Only relevant if Ka Ulukoa has unified/inclusive athletics component.
source_of_information: Web research - ESPN Take Back Sports initiative
special_notes: Requires inclusive/adaptive sports programming.

opportunity_name: Boys & Girls Clubs of America ALL STARS Program
funder_name: Boys & Girls Clubs of America / ESPN
mission_program_area: Multisport youth programming
brief_justification: ESPN partner for multisport programs. Requires BGCA affiliation. Consider partnership with Hawaii BGCA.
source_of_information: Web research - ESPN Take Back Sports initiative
special_notes: Must partner with local Boys & Girls Club.

opportunity_name: Gainbridge Assists (Parity/WSF)
mission_program_area: Girls and women in sports, education through athletics
brief_justification: $222K awarded to 32 orgs in 2025. Focus on girls/women athletes. If Ka Ulukoa serves female athletes, good fit.
special_notes: 2025 closed. Apply for 2026 cycle.

opportunity_name: Harry & Jeanette Weinberg Foundation
mission_program_area: Basic needs, community services, workforce development
brief_justification: Active in Hawaii. $500K recent grant to Aloha United Way. Not sports-specific but funds community facilities.
source_of_information: Web research - Hawaii nonprofit funding landscape
special_notes: Focus on low-income communities. Facility grants possible if serving economically disadvantaged youth.

opportunity_name: Enterprise Community Partners - Faith-Based Development Initiative
mission_program_area: Community facilities on faith-owned land
brief_justification: FACILITY: Community facilities focus. Only relevant if partnering with faith organization.
special_notes: Requires faith-based partnership. Could work if facility on church/temple land.

opportunity_name: Hawaii Tourism Authority Community Enrichment Program
mission_program_area: Community programs benefiting residents
brief_justification: State tourism funds community enrichment. Youth athletics benefits community. Worth inquiry.
special_notes: May require demonstrating tourism/community benefit connection.

opportunity_name: Ulupono Initiative - Community Grants
mission_program_area: Sustainable communities, food, energy, health
brief_justification: Hawaii-focused Omidyar initiative. Not sports-specific but supports community health/wellness.
special_notes: Primarily proactive grantmaking. Relationship building needed.

opportunity_name: Bank of Hawaii Foundation Grants
mission_program_area: Education, human services, arts, environment
brief_justification: Major Hawaii corporate foundation. Youth programs fit human services category.
special_notes: Check annual giving priorities for sports/youth athletics fit.

opportunity_name: First Hawaiian Bank Foundation Grants
mission_program_area: Education, health, human services, culture
brief_justification: Oldest Hawaii bank foundation. Youth development generally supported.
special_notes: Quarterly grant cycles - multiple opportunities per year.

opportunity_name: Alexander & Baldwin Foundation Grants
mission_program_area: Community development, education, environment
brief_justification: Major Hawaii company foundation. Community development includes youth programs.
special_notes: Check if facility development fits community development priorities.

opportunity_name: Samuel N. and Mary Castle Foundation
funder_name: Samuel N. and Mary Castle Foundation
mission_program_area: Education, human services, community development
brief_justification: Historic Hawaii foundation. Strong community focus. Human services includes youth programs.
special_notes: Related to Harold K.L. Castle Foundation. Research current giving priorities.
